g.text: Network Partition: the moment CAP forces a choice
Three nodes replicate a single key. The network splits Node C from the others. The same write 'x = 5' now produces two different system behaviors.
A ↔ B can still gossip and form a majority. C is isolated from the majority but still reachable by some clients.
• C catches up via log shipping / read-repair
Cost: clients connected to C see downtime for the duration of the partition.
• A, B see x = 5 ; C sees x = 9 → CONFLICT
• Reconcile by LWW timestamp, vector clocks,
CRDTs, or surface both versions to app.
Cost: replicas diverge during partition; application must handle conflict resolution.
The partition is the forcing function. No real distributed system is C+A all the time — every system has to declare what it does when the network splits.
